Description: Initial DeepMind AI for protein structure prediction; first major success at CASP13, establishing the field.
Revolutionary transformer-based architecture; massive leap in accuracy, foundational for all subsequent work.
Historic breakthrough solving the 50-year protein folding problem with near-experimental accuracy.
Key innovation using AF2 as a ranking tool; dramatically improved model selection and assessment.
CASP experiment shifting focus to protein complexes/multimers; benchmark for community progress.
Wallner lab method using massive stochastic sampling on AF2; top performer for multimers at CASP15.
CASP assessing AF3 and next-gen methods; highlighted performance plateaus on difficult targets.
Wallner extension generating conformational ensembles from AF2 via MSA masking; advanced dynamics prediction.
Advanced sampling framework on AF3 for better conformational state generation and selection.
Upcoming CASP round evaluating latest AI methods in an evolving field.
